opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I completely lost my voice this afternoon, and this tickle in my throat makes me cough until I retch.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: My voice went from croaky to completely gone in just a few hours and the coughing fits are so bad I gag — I wanted to check nothing serious is going on.

symptom_narrative.onset: It started about three days ago with a runny nose and feeling tired. This afternoon, over about six hours, my throat went really dry and scratchy and my voice faded to nothing.
symptom_narrative.duration: The voice loss came on over roughly six hours this afternoon; the cold feeling has been three days.
symptom_narrative.character: A completely lost voice — I can only force out a painful whisper. There is a constant dry tickle in my throat and a dry cough that makes me gag and retch.
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: The voice is totally gone and the coughing fits are really distressing, but I can still breathe fine.
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: In my throat and voice box area.
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: The tickle and cough come in fits, worse when I try to talk or clear my throat.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: Nothing much — I have tried throat lozenges but they do not help.
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: Trying to talk, and clearing my throat, which just sets off more coughing.
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: A bit of a sore throat when I swallow, mostly from all the throat-clearing. Runny nose earlier in the week.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get the odd croaky voice with colds but I have never completely lost it like this before.
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I cannot really speak, but luckily I do not need my voice for work for the next couple of days.

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Lives with family, low fall risk, no vocal demands at work for the next 48 hours.

objective_data_offered.value: Can only speak in a forced, painful whisper; frequent dry coughing fits during the call; breathing looks and sounds comfortable and quiet with no high-pitched or noisy breathing
Feels a bit warm/off but has not measured a temperature; not drooling and able to swallow saliva and sip water normally
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported impression, no home thermometer reading taken
